Tharun still main POC, will reconnect end of Q2 to schedule recap demo for all new stakeholders 5/19/26 GK: quick call, Tharun on PTO still - answered my call, said let's connect next Tuesday, sending tenative invite 5/11/26 GK: Tharun messaged last week on PTO. connecting 5/14 to discuss project timeline and realign on next steps 4/30/26 GK: scheduling 1:1 on with Tharun to discuss updates, timeline, and legal team involvement on next steps 4/22/26 GK: delays due to strategic team focused on Iran issues and price increases; tentative VP connect for 3/24 to discuss project timeline 3/11/26 GK: CLM deep dive with legal team, doing use case follow up. downselect pushed to end of April 3/4/26 GK: confirmed scope with Tharun via phone (iContract/iSource, possible ANA + Intake). prepping for CLM demo 3/11. coordinating call with VP for executive alignment in next 2 weeks 2/26/26 GK: AI call complete, sharing clarity on quote w/ AI. prepping for demo March 11th with legal team 2/23/26 GK: prepping for AI call; Want a full suite demo to possibly add more tools into scope of work for indirect spend, email to Tharun to schedule next call 12/3/25 GK: intro occurred, scope fluid, primary interest CLM/Spend/AI tools, multiple siloed tools in place currently for direct, indirect is a new addition to the team's responsibilities
hard.ais_why:
• Champion Tharun Chandirasekaran engaged the January 29 2026 demo on agentic capability across multiple dimensions. He pulled for custom AI prompt creation via the Merlin Agentic platform for end-to-end negotiation flows, asked for the rationale behind AI-suggested discount percentages, and requested a backend chemical supplier search via the autonomous agent. AI importance is rated Very Critical.
• Tharun raised concrete agentic-platform questions: surfacing Anna's negotiation rationale in the UI, Outlook integration for auto-syncing contract red-lines, RFX Scoring Pro automating supplier response scoring with AI, and AI-driven supplier identification via supplier.io. These are capability-fit probes, not generic curiosity. The discovery has moved past pitch into evaluation.
• Five completed meetings between December 2025 and March 2026 with Tharun as the consistent champion, plus legal counsel Chris Klein joining for contract module review and Ryan Anderson handling integration. Pricing has been quoted across iContract, iSource, iAnalyze, iRisk and ANA modules. With Very Critical AI posture and an agentic AI champion driving the relationship, this is a high-confidence Hungry deal.

north_star_verdict.headline: Strong technical evaluation (7 touchpoints, 6 demos, legal validated CLM) but on 26 May Tharun confirmed DuBois paused the project to Q3 to focus 100% on cost mitigation; the July 30 close cannot hold and there has been no buyer touch since 26 May.
north_star_verdict.math: Close 2026-07-30 is ~24 days out. Stage has been Formal Evaluation since ~5 Dec 2025 (~7 months in stage). Last verified buyer activity 2026-05-26 (~41 days ago); rep outreach on 6/22 and 6/29 targeting an early-July reconnect is still awaiting a buyer reply. The decision timeline has slipped four times (late-Feb to end-Q1 to end-Q2 to Q3) and the close date itself was pushed three times since March (4/23 to 5/20 to 6/16 to 7/2 to 7/30). The remaining path (Q3 recap demo, refreshed quote, EB/COO alignment, IT integration scoping, legal sandbox validation, commercials, contracting) cannot complete by 30 July; realistic close is Q4 2026.
evidence: Tharunkumar Chandirasekaran on the 26 May 2026 call: after the last demo 'the entire project kind of went into a pause... our focus kind of went 100% into cost mitigation and supply negotiations... the project was pushed to Q3'.
Next Step 6/1/26 (Grace Kim): 'next steps pushed to end of Q2; internally creating more guidelines for this project and looping in additional teams before follow up calls with vendors'.
Next Step 6/22/26 and 6/29/26: rep invited Tharun to a webinar and emailed to set July time, reconnect targeted early July, rep-initiated and awaiting a buyer reply.

Stage is still Formal Evaluation on a Best Case forecast, and the Decision Maker Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement) has not engaged since the 12 Dec 2025 discovery; a 19 Mar 2026 dial to her logged 'Could Not Connect'.

metrics.narrative: The operational scale is well established but never converted into a dollarized business case. On the 3 Dec 2025 intro call the buyer sized the problem at roughly $300-400M combined direct and indirect spend managed by a central sourcing team of about 9-10 people, and on 26 Feb Tharun made ROI an explicit decision gate: when opting for all three modules 'we want to see what is the return total investment and the return on investment, the integration complexity'. He was also explicit on 26 Feb that budget is the swing factor: 'if it's going out of budget, the AI part will be eliminated first'. Despite pricing being shared on 30 Jan and offered again on 26 May, no quantified ROI/savings model (time saved, compliance-risk reduced, maverick spend recaptured) has been delivered, so the value case remains qualitative. Building and presenting that quantified ROI is a ranked move and the wedge against do-nothing.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Jackie Zhang (Vice President Procurement, the SF primary Decision Maker), with the COO as the ultimate approver: on 26 Feb Tharun described the structure as 'we function as a lead, like VP and then the sourcing managers' and confirmed 'our COO will be there... the VP will be reporting to her', adding that the team will be 'pushing the project with the C-suite for the budget'. Tharun repeatedly places the decision above himself ('I'll be providing my feedback to my VP'). The gap is engagement, not identification: Jackie has not been on a call since 12 Dec 2025, a 19 Mar 2026 dial to her logged 'Could Not Connect', and a Grace-to-Jackie / Mike-to-Jackie intro has been pending since March and never happened. The COO has never been named or engaged. Re-securing the Jackie alignment call is the ranked move to reactivate the economic buyer.
sources: Task 19 Mar 2026 (Call Jackie Zhang, Could Not Connect)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and multi-sourced. For CLM, Chris Klein set efficiency as the primary bar on 11 Mar: 'how can it be done as efficiently as possible... as minimal clicks and inputs as possible', and his acceptance test is accuracy of AI contract extraction on DuBois's own sample agreements ('do samples... to see the accuracy of the extraction'). Laura confirmed the same gate: 'it'll be very helpful when we provide a sample and see the accuracy, that's really key'. On 26 Feb Tharun named the three commercial criteria for going all-in: total ROI, integration complexity with the existing ecosystem, and staying within budget, with a stated fallback to split CLM and sourcing across two vendors if the suite exceeds budget. Legal ease-of-use carries 'equal weightage' to sourcing in the decision (Tharun, 26 Feb).
sources: Avoma CLM deep dive 11 Mar 2026 (Chris Klein, Laura)

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, DuBois is running a sourcing-team-led evaluation: Tharun (Sourcing Analyst / super-user) has owned every touchpoint, brought in legal (Chris Klein, Laura) on the 11 Mar CLM deep dive and IT (Ryan Anderson) for integration, and feeds a recommendation up to the relevant stakeholder Zhang, with COO sign-off and a C-suite budget push above her. On 26 Feb Tharun described a shortlist-to-top-3 process ('interviewing 10 different vendors, consolidate to top 3 by end of first quarter') followed by a full-team demo with the VP and sourcing leaders. That process is now disrupted: on 26 May Tharun said a new company-wide IT-prioritization lead and an internal approval workflow have been introduced and the project is paused to Q3 while the team 're-grounds' its requirements. The near-term process is therefore an internal re-scoping the buyer controls, not a vendor-facing step.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have opened, which is stage-appropriate for a paused Formal Evaluation. The only paper-adjacent signals are that DuBois uses DocuSign as its e-signature tool (Laura, 11 Mar) and that a legal validation step (sandbox testing of contract extraction on DuBois sample agreements) is the buyer's gate before commercial commitment. No MSA/SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or legal redline motion exists yet. Visibility will come only once the buyer resumes in Q3 and a recap demo plus refreshed quote re-anchor the evaluation; asking Tharun for the internal approval-workflow steps the new IT lead has introduced would surface the real path to signature.
sources: Avoma CLM deep dive 11 Mar 2026 (Laura, DocuSign)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific, owned by the sourcing team, and articulated by the relevant stakeholder the 12 Dec 2025 discovery and reinforced on 11 Mar, DuBois has no real contract-management tooling: contracts are tracked manually with no expiry alerts and no spend-to-contract utilization tracking, reports are hand-linked through a custom internal Power BI, and indirect spend (a new team responsibility after acquisitions) has no approved vendor list and runs on manual email-based RFQs. Chris Klein voiced the CLM pain viscerally on 11 Mar: prior platforms 'added significantly more time and work', and his dream is 'I have a contract, I press submit or I upload it, and I'm done... two clicks' rather than typing 25 fields. The pain owners are Tharun (sourcing/productivity) and Chris/Laura (legal contract efficiency); it is confirmed, not inferred.
sources: Avoma CLM deep dive 11 Mar 2026 (Chris Klein)

champion.narrative: Tharunkumar Chandirasekaran (Sourcing Analyst, self-described super-user and project POC) is an active, communicative champion: he has driven all seven touchpoints, produced the requirements checklist, looped in IT (Ryan Anderson) and legal (Chris Klein, Laura), and on 26 May proactively reassured Zycus during the pause: 'it's not like we are resuming the project pursuing this with a different supplier'. He also stated a personal preference on 11 Mar: 'I personally like the platform'. But he is a developing champion, not a power champion: he has no budget authority, cannot close on his own ('I'll be providing my feedback to my VP'), and his access to Jackie Zhang and the COO has not converted into any executive engagement. What has developed: deep technical advocacy and continuous, candid communication. What is still missing: an executive-sponsor relationship and any proof he can actually move Jackie or unlock budget.

competition.narrative: The dominant current threat is the status quo / do-nothing path: on 26 May the project was paused with focus shifted '100% into cost mitigation and supply negotiations', and DuBois runs a mature incumbent stack (Dynamics 365 ERP, Coupa for supplier onboarding and invoicing, SAP Ariba/Blackstone for e-bidding, custom Power BI for analytics) it is not rushing to replace. Coupa is the medium live threat for the AI/sourcing scope: on 26 Feb Zycus's own consultant Bikash observed Coupa 'already have the supplier piece... maybe a natural choice'. A two-vendor split is a live structural risk: on 26 Feb Tharun said that if CLM exceeds budget they 'would think about diversifying the project between two different vendors, one taking CLM, the other sourcing'. SAP Ariba is a low-threat incumbent confined to e-bidding. No rival CLM suite vendor has been named on any call. Zycus wins by proving quantified ROI to beat inertia and single-suite TCO to prevent the split, anchored on the CLM gap no incumbent fills.

deal_movement.summary: Deep technical progress through Q1 (five demos Dec-Mar, legal CLM deep dive on 11 Mar, pricing shared 30 Jan) stalled in Q2 when DuBois paused the project to Q3 for cost mitigation. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 26 May recap-planning call; since then only rep-side outreach (6/1, 6/11, 6/22, 6/29) awaiting a reply, plus three close-date pushes and a division re-tag on 6/30.

items.change: Buyer paused the project to Q3, redirecting focus 100% to cost mitigation; Tharun confirmed on the recap-planning call.
Close date pushed 6/16 to 7/2 then 7/2 to 7/30 by Grace Kim.
CLM deep dive with legal (Chris Klein, Laura) completed; iContract AI extraction and DocuSign integration validated.
Pricing shared across iContract, iSource and analytics modules; AI deep dive scheduled.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo / do-nothing path: as of 26 May 2026 the project is paused with focus 100% on cost mitigation, and DuBois retains a mature Coupa + Ariba + Dynamics 365 + Power BI stack it will not rush to replace. Coupa is the medium live threat for the AI/sourcing scope (Zycus's own consultant called it the 'natural choice' on 26 Feb). A two-vendor split (CLM to one vendor, sourcing to another) is a medium structural risk if CLM exceeds budget. SAP Ariba is a low-threat incumbent confined to e-bidding. No rival CLM suite vendor has surfaced on any call.

competitors.name: Status Quo / Do-Nothing
competitors.quote: our focus kind of went 100% into cost mitigation and supply negotiations... the project was pushed to Q3
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver a quantified ROI model (savings, compliance-risk reduction, maverick-spend capture on $300-400M spend across 9-10 FTEs) so the suite beats inertia, and re-anchor on the CLM gap no incumbent fills.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I would say maybe a natural choice if I had been in your place would be to go for Coupa than looking for a third party solution
competitors.how_we_win: Position iContract + iSource + Merlin agentic AI integrating with Coupa (not replacing onboarding/invoicing) and show Coupa cannot match the CLM depth and autonomous negotiation.

competitors.name: Two-vendor split (CLM and sourcing to different vendors)
competitors.quote: we would think about diversifying the project between two different vendors. One would be taking the CLM, the other would be taking the sourcing
competitors.how_we_win: Prove suite synergy and single-vendor TCO advantage; price CLM within budget so both modules stay with Zycus.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Existing e-bidding via Ariba and invoice payments through Coupa on top of Dynamics 365 ERP
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is confined to e-bidding via Blackstone; highlight Zycus's integrated source-to-pay + CLM coverage Ariba does not provide here.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits the stated CLM efficiency, AI extraction, and agentic-sourcing criteria well and legal reacted positively to iContract on 11 Mar; the exposure is commercial (ROI/budget) and the buyer's own paused, re-scoping process, not product fit.

items.quote: I have a contract, I press submit or I upload it, and I'm done... two clicks
items.source: Avoma CLM deep dive 11 Mar 2026 (Chris Klein)

items.criterion: Agentic AI across sourcing and analytics (autonomous negotiation, conversational spend queries)

items.criterion: Quantified ROI and integration complexity within budget for all three modules
items.quote: we want to see what is the return total investment and the return on investment, the integration complexity... if it's going out of budget, the AI part will be eliminated first

items.requirement: Deliver a recap demo of the sourcing and CLM tools when DuBois resumes in Q3.
items.quote: when we are trying to resume it, we would love a recap of what we went through so far and where we stand at this point

items.deliverable: Share a refreshed pricing quote alongside the Q3 recap demo so DuBois sees initial pricing on the current scope.
items.grounding_quote: once we sort of reconnect, do a recap demo and maybe even share a quote just so that you guys can see that initial pricing as well

items.deliverable: Signal the Q3 resume date and confirm the refined module scope after the internal re-prioritization.
items.grounding_quote: some of the points that we mentioned earlier may be removed... we may no longer need those specific features

items.quote: we want to see what is the return total investment and the return on investment
Call Jackie Zhang... Could Not Connect

items.area: Q3 resume date and final module scope after re-prioritization
items.quote: the entire project kind of went into a pause... pushed to Q3

best_practice_check.summary: A strong, engaged champion carried a deep technical evaluation, but the deal is paused with no executive access and no dollarized value case. The three highest-impact levers now are converting pain to ROI, reactivating the economic buyer, and locking scope before the vendor split forms.
flags: Build and land a quantified ROI model on the $300-400M spend / 9-10 FTE base to beat the do-nothing pause and justify the full suite over a two-vendor split.
Reactivate the economic buyer: convert Tharun's access into the long-pending Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement) alignment call before the Q3 recap, since the deal cannot advance without her and the COO.

Lock scope with Tharun before the recap demo: the buyer flagged features may be dropped and AI eliminated first if over budget, so confirm which modules survive to price accurately and protect the suite.

items.name: Jackie Zhang
items.title: Vice President Procurement
items.risk: Holds budget authority and is the SF primary Decision Maker but has been disengaged since Dec 2025; a 19 Mar dial logged Could Not Connect. The deal cannot advance without her.

items.name: Tharunkumar Chandirasekaran
items.title: Sourcing Analyst
items.risk: Active, communicative POC and super-user but no budget authority; developing, not yet a power champion, and cannot close alone.

items.name: Chris Klein
items.title: Technical Representative
items.risk: Legal evaluator with equal-weight CLM input; efficiency-skeptical and gates on sample-contract extraction accuracy; recommendation unconfirmed.

items.name: Ryan Anderson
items.title: Enterprise Resources Planning Business Analyst
items.risk: IT/integration contact who assesses integration complexity with the Dynamics 365 ecosystem; not yet actively engaged.

champion_strength.summary: Tharun is an active, candid champion who drove the whole evaluation, looped in IT and legal, and personally likes the platform, but he is developing not a power champion: no budget authority, cannot move the economic buyer, and the executive layer (Jackie, COO) is still unengaged.
champion_strength.source: All 7 Avoma touchpoints; Avoma tentative connect 26 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/agentic story is landing well: Tharun drove deep agentic-platform questions (natural-language prompt agents, autonomous negotiation, conversational spend analytics) on 26 Jan and 26 Feb, and AIS is Strong (8/10). The risk is commercial, not narrative: AI is the first module the buyer would cut if over budget.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8.0 / Strong is corroborated by the calls: Tharun probed agentic flows, natural-language prompt agents, autonomous negotiation and conversational analytics in depth on 26 Jan and 26 Feb, rating AI Very Critical. The field is accurately rated, though budget pressure could demote AI to a later phase.

items.detail: Project paused to Q3 with focus 100% on cost mitigation; AI would be eliminated first if the suite exceeds budget, and CLM/sourcing could split across two vendors.
items.source: Avoma AI discussion 26 Feb 2026; Avoma tentative connect 26 May 2026

items.detail: Economic buyer Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement) and the COO above her have never been engaged; the developing champion cannot reach power.
Decision timeline has slipped four times and the close date three times; a new internal IT-prioritization lead and approval workflow reset the buyer's process.
items.source: Field history; Avoma tentative connect 26 May 2026

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled: the last genuine two-way buyer exchange was 26 May, followed only by rep-side outreach awaiting a reply. The champion stays warm and communicative, so this is a paused deal, not a lost one, but momentum is clearly thinning.
items: No buyer-facing deliverable executed since the 26 May recap-planning call.
Champion Tharun remains responsive and reassuring, keeping the relationship warm through the pause.
Four rep-initiated touches (6/1, 6/11, 6/22, 6/29) with no substantive buyer reply logged.

items.action: Reconnect with Tharun to schedule the Q3 recap demo and refreshed quote.
items.trigger: Tharun asked for a recap when DuBois resumes; rep outreach on 6/22 and 6/29 targeted early July with no reply yet.
items.expected_effect: Restarts momentum out of the Q3 pause and re-opens the evaluation before the buyer's re-scoping hardens.

items.action: Build and present a quantified ROI model on DuBois's $300-400M spend and 9-10 FTEs.
items.trigger: Tharun made total ROI an explicit decision gate on 26 Feb and cost the reason for the pause on 26 May, yet no savings model has been delivered.
items.expected_effect: Beats the do-nothing / cost-mitigation inertia and defends the full suite against the two-vendor split.

items.action: Have Tharun secure the pending Jackie Zhang alignment call to re-engage the economic buyer.
items.trigger: Jackie (VP Procurement, budget owner) has not engaged since 12 Dec; a Mike-to-Jackie intro has been pending since March.
items.expected_effect: Gives the deal a path to budget approval it currently lacks and tests the champion's reach into power.

items.action: Confirm with Tharun which modules survive the re-scoping before pricing the recap.
items.trigger: On 26 May Tharun warned some features may be removed and on 26 Feb said AI is cut first if over budget.
items.expected_effect: Lets Zycus price accurately, protect the suite, and pre-empt a two-vendor split.

items.action: Run a Coupa-vs-suite framing showing single-vendor TCO and the CLM gap Coupa cannot fill.
items.trigger: Zycus's own consultant flagged Coupa as the 'natural choice' on 26 Feb and DuBois runs Coupa for onboarding/invoicing.
items.expected_effect: Neutralizes the incumbent-Coupa pull and reinforces why the integrated suite wins the AI/sourcing scope.

scope_change.from: Full suite: CLM (iContract) + Sourcing (iSource, analytics, RFX) + AI/Merlin agentic layer across all modules, evaluated together.
scope_change.to: Sourcing-led scope with CLM secondary; AI to be dropped first if over budget, some previously-scoped features may be removed, and a possible split of CLM and sourcing across two vendors.
scope_change.detail: On 26 May Tharun said focus 'should be more on the sourcing side' and 'some of the points we mentioned earlier may be removed'; on 26 Feb he said if over budget 'the AI part will be eliminated first' and they would otherwise 'diversify the project between two different vendors'. This defensive narrowing tracks the cost-mitigation pause.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A strong-fit, AI-hungry evaluation carried by a developing champion has stalled: DuBois paused the project to Q3 for cost mitigation, the economic buyer is unengaged, and scope is narrowing defensively, so the only thing that reopens the win is a quantified ROI case that beats do-nothing.

win_position.text: Scores in the low-50s and not higher because, despite a Strong AIS and a legal-validated CLM fit, this is a Formal Evaluation the buyer paused to Q3 with the economic buyer (Jackie Zhang) unengaged since December.

Product fit is genuine: Chris Klein and Laura reacted positively to iContract's two-click extraction on 11 Mar, and Tharun said 'I personally like the platform' while driving deep agentic-AI questions on 26 Feb.
The top threat is inertia, not a rival: on 26 May Tharun redirected focus '100% into cost mitigation', and with no ROI model delivered the do-nothing path is currently winning.
Scope is narrowing defensively: AI would be cut first if over budget and the buyer floated splitting CLM and sourcing across two vendors, so the full-suite number is at risk.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: the last genuine two-way buyer exchange was 26 May, followed only by four rep-side touches (6/1, 6/11, 6/22, 6/29) awaiting a reply.
The decision timeline has slipped four times (late-Feb to end-Q1 to end-Q2 to Q3) and the close date three times since March, now 30 July against a paused project.
Champion Tharun stays responsive and reassuring through the pause ('not pursuing this with a different supplier'), keeping the relationship warm for a Q3 restart.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invested in seven touchpoints and six demos, brought legal and IT into a 90-minute CLM deep dive, and made total ROI an explicit go/no-go gate on 26 Feb.
Commitment is capped below decision-maker level: Tharun defers to his VP and the COO for budget, and neither has ever engaged, so no power commitment exists.
The buyer's own re-scoping (new IT-prioritization lead, internal approval workflow) means near-term commitment is to an internal reset, not a vendor step.

deal_risk.text: Budget risk is primary: the project paused for cost mitigation and AI is the first module to be cut if the suite runs over.
Executive-access risk: the economic buyer Jackie Zhang and the COO above her have never engaged, and a 19 Mar dial to Jackie logged Could Not Connect.
Structural risk: a two-vendor split of CLM and sourcing would break the suite economics if Zycus cannot price CLM within budget.

close_plan.evidence: Decision timeline slipped four times and the project is paused to Q3 with no concrete customer-owned date (26 May).

economic_buyer_engagement.evidence: Jackie Zhang unengaged since 12 Dec 2025; 19 Mar dial Could Not Connect; COO never engaged.

budget_risk.evidence: Project paused for cost mitigation; AI eliminated first if over budget (26 Feb, 26 May).

customer_preference.evidence: Tharun said 'I personally like the platform' (11 Mar) and drove Zycus-specific agentic-AI questions, but no decisive lean over Coupa/two-vendor split and the project is paused.

business_case.evidence: Pain and scale are quantified ($300-400M spend, 9-10 FTEs, manual RFQs, no contract alerts) but no ROI/savings model has been built; ROI is an explicit buyer decision gate still unmet.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 26 May Tharun said focus 'should be more on the sourcing side' and 'some of the points we mentioned earlier may be removed'; on 26 Feb he said if over budget (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 36 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 29 is carried by momentum 57 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — On 26 May Tharun said focus 'should be more on the sourcing side' and 'some of the points we mentioned earlier….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 57 (moving forward): Next Step carries 36 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (29), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 57, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: DuBois Chemicals Inc. — Formal Evaluation · $115K · closes 30 Jul
cro_panel.intro: A strong-fit, AI-hungry evaluation carried by a developing champion has stalled: DuBois paused the project to Q3 for cost mitigation, the economic buyer is unengaged, and scope is narrowing defensively, so the only thing that reopens the win is a quantified ROI case that beats do-nothing.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.text: Scores in the low-50s and not higher because, despite a Strong AIS and a legal-validated CLM fit, this is a Formal Evaluation the buyer paused to Q3 with the economic buyer (Jackie Zhang) unengaged since December.

Product fit is genuine: Chris Klein and Laura reacted positively to iContract's two-click extraction on 11 Mar, and Tharun said 'I personally like the platform' while driving deep agentic-AI questions on 26 Feb.
The top threat is inertia, not a rival: on 26 May Tharun redirected focus '100% into cost mitigation', and with no ROI model delivered the do-nothing path is currently winning.
Scope is narrowing defensively: AI would be cut first if over budget and the buyer floated splitting CLM and sourcing across two vendors, so the full-suite number is at risk.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 29.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum has stalled: the last genuine two-way buyer exchange was 26 May, followed only by four rep-side touches (6/1, 6/11, 6/22, 6/29) awaiting a reply.
The decision timeline has slipped four times (late-Feb to end-Q1 to end-Q2 to Q3) and the close date three times since March, now 30 July against a paused project.
Champion Tharun stays responsive and reassuring through the pause ('not pursuing this with a different supplier'), keeping the relationship warm for a Q3 restart.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Budget risk is primary: the project paused for cost mitigation and AI is the first module to be cut if the suite runs over.
Executive-access risk: the economic buyer Jackie Zhang and the COO above her have never engaged, and a 19 Mar dial to Jackie logged Could Not Connect.
Structural risk: a two-vendor split of CLM and sourcing would break the suite economics if Zycus cannot price CLM within budget.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: The single strongest CURRENT threat is the status quo / do-nothing path: as of 26 May 2026 the project is paused with focus 100% on cost mitigation, and DuBois retains a mature Coupa + Ariba + Dynamics 365 + Power BI stack it will not rush…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: The buyer invested in seven touchpoints and six demos, brought legal and IT into a 90-minute CLM deep dive, and made total ROI an explicit go/no-go gate on 26 Feb.
Commitment is capped below decision-maker level: Tharun defers to his VP and the COO for budget, and neither has ever engaged, so no power commitment exists.
The buyer's own re-scoping (new IT-prioritization lead, internal approval workflow) means near-term commitment is to an internal reset, not a vendor step.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Reconnect with Tharun to schedule the Q3 recap demo and refreshed quote. (by 13 Jul)
Build and present a quantified ROI model on DuBois's $300-400M spend and 9-10 FTEs. (by 20 Jul)
Have Tharun secure the pending Jackie Zhang alignment call to re-engage the economic buyer. (by 22 Jul)

gaps: No quantified ROI/savings business case delivered despite it being an explicit buyer decision gate (26 Feb).
Economic buyer Jackie Zhang and the COO above her have never engaged on a call.
Q3 resume date and final post-re-scoping module scope are unconfirmed.
